FORD MOTOR COMPANY
Collision Repair Procedures

2022 FORD F-150
SuperCrew / SuperCab / Regular Cab

REAR QUARTER / BED SIDE OUTER PANEL REPLACEMENT

SECTION: BODY REPAIR — REAR BODY SIDE

GENERAL PRECAUTIONS

WARNING: The 2022 F-150 uses military-grade aluminum alloy body panels
(6000-series aluminum). Standard steel repair equipment is NOT compatible.
Use only aluminum-certified repair equipment and tools.

CAUTION: Aluminum dust is a fire and explosion hazard. Ensure adequate
ventilation and use explosion-proof equipment.

REMOVAL PROCEDURE

1. Preparation
   a. Place vehicle on frame straightening equipment if structural damage present
   b. Disconnect negative battery cable
   c. Depressurize air suspension system if equipped (2022 F-150 PowerBoost models)

2. Trim and Component Removal
   Remove bed extender (if equipped), tailgate, and all bed accessories.
   Remove rear combination lamp assemblies (four 8mm bolts each side).
   Remove wheel arch liner — eight clips and three 8mm bolts.

3. Bed Side Panel Removal
   NOTE: Aluminum bed side panels are attached using a combination of
   aluminum rivets and structural adhesive. Do NOT attempt to spot weld
   aluminum panels without aluminum-capable resistance welding equipment.

   Drill out aluminum rivets using 5/16" cobalt bit (never carbon steel bits).
   Use heat-resistant adhesive release solvent on bonded flanges.
   Apply controlled heat (max 120°C) to soften adhesive if required.

INSTALLATION PROCEDURE

4. Panel Preparation
   Clean all flanges with aluminum-safe cleaner (no iron contamination).
   Apply self-etching primer approved for aluminum substrate.
   DO NOT use steel weld-through primer on aluminum panels.

5. Adhesive Application
   Apply Ford-approved structural adhesive (STA 36960-A or equivalent) to
   all bonded flange areas. Bead width: 6-8mm continuous bead.
   Clamp or rivet within working time of adhesive per product instructions.

6. Riveting
   Install aluminum rivets per factory rivet map.
   Rivet type: aluminum blind rivet, 5/16" diameter
   Pitch: 25mm center-to-center at structural flanges

7. Gap and Reveal Verification
   Verify all panel gaps meet specifications:
   - Bed side to cab: 8.0mm +/- 2.0mm
   - Bed side to wheel arch: flush +/- 1.5mm

Advisory: This procedure covers the general body panel replacement process.
Always refer to Ford's current Workshop Manual for the specific VIN and
production date of the vehicle being repaired.
